steps.title: Read the vertex from vertex form
steps.content: The function $f(x) = \frac{1}{8}(x - 3)^2 + 6$ is in vertex form $a(x - h)^2 + k$. Matching: $h = 3$, $k = 6$. Vertex = $(3,\,6)$.

steps.title: Determine whether the vertex is a minimum or maximum
steps.content: The leading coefficient $a = \frac{1}{8} \gt 0$, so the parabola opens upward. The vertex is the minimum point of the function.

steps.title: Interpret the vertex in context
steps.content: The vertex $(3,\,6)$ means: at $x = 3$ seconds, the drone's height is $f(3) = 6$ inches — the minimum height on the track. That is the correct answer.

choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — The toy drone's minimum height was 6 inches above the ground.: The vertex is $(3, 6)$ and $a \gt 0$ makes it a minimum; $f = 6$ inches is the minimum height. ✓
  • Choice B — The toy drone's minimum height was 3 inches above the ground.: Confuses $h = 3$ (the time in seconds) with the height value; the height at the vertex is 6 inches, not 3. ✗
  • Choice C — The toy drone's height was 6 inches above the ground when it started moving.: The drone started at $x = 0$, giving $f(0) = \frac{1}{8}(0-3)^2 + 6 = \frac{9}{8} + 6 = 7.125$ inches, not 6 inches. ✗
  • Choice D — The toy drone's height was 3 inches above the ground when it started moving.: Neither the starting height nor 3 as a height value is supported by the function. ✗
That is the correct answer.
